feature_source.runtime: DirectML on the tracked AMD host

ai_contract.matched_ablation: Append the already generated, hash-verified causal FinCast features to the identical support-aware hurdle model. Reuse is mandatory; this round may not rerun the 3,462-second foundation-model extraction.
ai_contract.numeric_authority: FinCast supplies causal numeric forecast features only. It cannot select a threshold, submit or size an order, approve leverage, or override a deterministic risk gate.
ai_contract.uplift_rule: AI is retained only if it improves executable-row probability log loss and expected-payoff rank, and improves paired base and stress economics without worsening drawdown or concentration. A mixed result fails.

chronological_roles.evaluation: 2023-06-09 through 2023-06-14 UTC; already consumed and diagnostic only
chronological_roles.role_boundary_rule: Every feature, entry, path quote, exit, and label must finish before the next role starts. Side-specific exits are purged independently.

claims.reason: The complete interval was inspected in Round 51. Round 52 is a consumed-development mechanism screen and cannot confirm generalization, profitability, or AI uplift.

data_contract.event_source: Official Binance Data Vision USD-M daily archives with checksum-verified warehouse ingestion
data_contract.historical_depth_limit: bookDepth is sampled aggregate percentage-band depth, not full L2 queue evidence. Passive-fill or queue-position claims are prohibited.

economic_screen.stress_rule: Reprice the exact base ledger with the frozen stress protection delay and two additional basis points per side. Never reselect trades under stress.
economic_screen.threshold_selection: For each fixed coverage and symbol, derive a numeric worst-seed expected-net-bps threshold from policy-calibration predictions only. Select the passing coverage with the highest paired-stress mean; break ties by lower drawdown, then lower coverage. If none passes, the candidate abstains on evaluation.
economic_screen.trade_rule: A side must be replay-executable, every seed must predict positive after-cost utility, and ensemble profitable probability must exceed 0.50. Choose the side with the greater worst-seed expected utility; ties abstain. Apply only the frozen numeric threshold learned before evaluation.

execution_target.entry_and_exit: Cross the contemporaneous ask to enter long and bid to exit long; reverse for short. Enforce observed quote age, side-specific L1 participation, latency, same-day exit, and one non-overlapping position per symbol.

architectures.target: Separate side-specific conditional means of exact after-cost net bps, trained only where that side is replay-executable.

For each side, estimate profitable-event probability, conditional positive payoff mean, and conditional non-profitable loss magnitude only on replay-executable rows; recombine them by the law of total expectation.

architectures.single_change: Append the frozen causal FinCast matrix to the identical executable hurdle candidate.

model_contract.conditional_mean_objective: Unweighted squared error because the conditional arithmetic mean is required for expected utility. Negative magnitude predictions are floored at zero only after prediction and are counted as numerical defects.

lightgbm.minimum_leaf_rows: max(64, min(512, ceil(0.002 * side-specific training rows)))

model_contract.probability_calibration: One bounded Platt map per side and seed, fitted only on the dedicated probability-calibration role after side-specific support and boundary purging.
model_contract.replay_executable_support: target valid AND selected side L1 participation <= 10% AND selected side target exit remains in the same UTC day. Quote-age eligibility is already enforced during feature construction.
model_contract.support_invariant: Training, early stopping, probability calibration, policy calibration, proper-score baselines, threshold derivation, and evaluation must use the same side-specific replay-executable predicate.

objective: Determine whether correcting Round 51's target-policy support mismatch and decomposing exact executable payoff into probability and conditional severity creates a stable after-cost tail, and whether cached FinCast features add incremental value over the matched deterministic model.

ai: FinCast must improve mean executable-row binary log-loss skill and expected-payoff Spearman by at least 0.005 over the matched hurdle control.
FinCast must improve both base and paired-stress policy economics without worse drawdown, concentration, or trade count.
AI cannot pass when the deterministic hurdle candidate fails numerical or economic gates.
economic: Thresholds and coverage are selected only on policy calibration; evaluation cannot choose or repair them.
Evaluation requires at least 30 non-overlapping closed trades, two positive symbols, bounded concentration, positive base and paired-stress return, and profit factor above one.
No leverage, ROI annualization, or live authority is permitted in this consumed screen.
numerical: All source artifacts, support masks, role indexes, labels, models, predictions, and ledgers are finite and hash-bound.
Every side and role records total valid rows, executable rows, rejected rows, class support, and exact mask SHA-256.
Every model reloads exactly; OpenCL records no CPU fallback; unsupported rows can never enter model fitting, calibration, threshold selection, scoring, or replay.
predictive: Positive executable-row binary-log-loss and Brier skill versus training prevalence for both sides of every symbol.
Positive expected-payoff MSE skill versus the executable training mean and minimum expected-payoff Spearman 0.03 for both sides of every symbol.
Minimum pairwise seed expected-payoff Spearman 0.50 and no seed with negative top-tail calibration economics.

research_basis.limitation: The study uses hourly BTC and selected configurations; its performance is not imported into this repository.
research_basis.use: Recent walk-forward BTC evidence finds that naive sign trading fails after ten-basis-point costs and that cost-aware execution filtering is central to economic viability.

research_basis.limitation: The paper studies monthly equity returns; Round 52 tests an analogous decomposition on exact five-minute executable crypto payoffs.
research_basis.use: Separating sign and magnitude can capture nonlinear return structure and supplies a current financial basis for explicit probability-and-severity modeling.

research_basis.limitation: TLOB uses richer full order books than the sampled aggregate depth available here, so its architecture is not copied.
research_basis.use: Simple models can remain competitive with complex LOB networks, while spread-aware labels materially reduce apparent predictability. Complexity is deferred until a support-correct baseline passes.

research_basis.limitation: The paper studies NASDAQ LOBs rather than Binance futures.
research_basis.use: High forecast accuracy does not imply actionable trading signals; evaluation must model complete executable transactions.

research_basis.limitation: The theory is generic regression and does not establish trading profitability.
research_basis.use: Selective regression supports abstention under heteroskedastic uncertainty rather than forcing a prediction or a trade at every row.

research_basis.limitation: The engine cannot reconstruct queue evidence absent from the historical source.
research_basis.use: Credible passive-order replay requires latency and queue-position models over full depth, so Round 52 remains conservative taker execution.
